questions: You always say exactly what you mean, and people trust you because your words match your actions. Your neighbor asks if you've noticed any problems with their new fence that is actually on your property line.
You always say exactly what you mean, and people trust you because your words match your actions. A colleague asks if you applied for the same promotion they're going for.
You always say exactly what you mean, and people trust you because your words match your actions. Your sister asks if you told your parents about her new tattoo.
You always say exactly what you mean, and people trust you because your words match your actions. Your roommate asks if you used the last of the coffee this morning.
You always say exactly what you mean, and people trust you because your words match your actions. A friend asks if you've been to the new restaurant on Main Street yet.
You always say exactly what you mean, and people trust you because your words match your actions. Your coworker asks if you finished the section of the report you were assigned.
You always say exactly what you mean, and people trust you because your words match your actions. A teammate asks if you've started looking into the issue the client flagged last week.
You always say exactly what you mean, and people trust you because your words match your actions. Your friend asks if you're still planning to help them move this Saturday.
You always say exactly what you mean, and people trust you because your words match your actions. A neighbor asks if you know who left the trash bins out on the sidewalk past collection day.
You always say exactly what you mean, and people trust you because your words match your actions. Your manager asks if you've had a chance to review the draft they shared yesterday.
